column1: # xlsx right-side borders paint a box-drawing glyph on the rightmost
# column of each cell's slot.  Style → glyph mapping:
# Top/bottom borders are *preserved* on round-trip but not yet
# rendered; this transcript covers the v1 right-edge slice.
# Pointer suppression: the highlighted cell drops its border so the
# REVERSED selection stays loud.  Move pointer off A1..E1 first.
# E1 has no border — rightmost column is whatever padding the cell
# rendered (a space for short text in default-width column).
# Park the pointer on B1 — its border should disappear (suppressed),
# and the cell renders a plain space at the right edge.  Adjacent A1
# and C1 keep their borders since they're not highlighted.
